_meta.description: UNFPA OTG Field App Drug Formulary — reproductive health essential medicines
_meta.note: SCAFFOLD ONLY. All entries below must be verified by a qualified clinician and approved by the Clinical Advisory Panel before deployment. Source citations must reference exact page numbers.
sources: WHO Model List of Essential Medicines 23rd edition (2023)
WHO PCPNC — Pregnancy, Childbirth, Postpartum and Newborn Care: A guide for essential practice (3rd ed, 2023)
WHO IMPAC/MCPC — Managing Complications in Pregnancy and Childbirth (2nd ed, 2017)
WHO Medical Eligibility Criteria for Contraceptive Use (MEC) 5th edition (2015)

localNames.my: အောက်စီတိုစင်
localNames.km: អុកស៊ីតូស៊ីន

drugs.indication: Active management of third stage of labour (AMTSL); prevention and treatment of postpartum haemorrhage (PPH)
drugs.timing: Within 1 minute of delivery of the baby, before delivery of the placenta
drugs.alternativeDose: For PPH treatment: 20–40 IU in 1 litre IV fluids at 40 drops/min; or 10 IU IM if IV access not available
contraindications: Situations where vaginal delivery is inadvisable
warnings: Do not give as rapid IV bolus — may cause hypotension and cardiac arrhythmia
Has antidiuretic effect — risk of water intoxication with prolonged use in large volumes
Store at 2–8°C; loses potency above 25°C — in hot climates, prefer misoprostol if cold chain unreliable
drugs.source: WHO PCPNC 3rd ed. 2023, Section C3 (Preventing postpartum haemorrhage), Page 89

localNames.km: មីស្សូប្រូស្តូល

drugs.indication: Prevention of PPH where oxytocin unavailable or cold chain unreliable; cervical ripening; early pregnancy failure; incomplete abortion
drugs.timing: Immediately after delivery of the baby
drugs.alternativeDose: PPH treatment (when oxytocin unavailable): 800 mcg sublingually. Cervical priming: 25 mcg vaginally q6h (hospital setting only).
contraindications: Previous uterine scar with >1 prior caesarean (for cervical ripening doses)
warnings: Side effects: fever, shivering, nausea, diarrhoea — inform patient before use
Higher doses (800 mcg SL) for PPH treatment have more side effects — clinical supervision required
Do not use sublingual doses for PPH prevention in facility settings when oxytocin is available
Legal context varies by country — verify local regulations before use for abortion indications
drugs.source: WHO PCPNC 3rd ed. 2023, Section C3; WHO IMPAC/MCPC 2nd ed. 2017

localNames.my: မဂ္ဂနီဆီယမ် ဆာလဖိတ်
localNames.km: ម៉ាញ៉េស្យូម ស៊ុលហ្វាត

drugs.indication: Prevention and treatment of eclamptic seizures; severe pre-eclampsia
drugs.dose: Loading: 4 g IV over 5–10 minutes, then 1 g/hour IV maintenance
drugs.route: IV (or IM if IV access unavailable — see alternative)
drugs.timing: Start immediately on diagnosis of severe pre-eclampsia or eclampsia
drugs.alternativeDose: IM loading (if no IV): 5 g IM each buttock (= 10 g total) + 2.5 g IM q4h. Continue for 24 hours after last seizure or delivery.
contraindications: Urine output < 25 mL/hour in preceding 4 hours
warnings: Have calcium gluconate 1 g IV ready as antidote for magnesium toxicity
Monitor: respiratory rate, patellar reflexes, and urine output every 30 minutes
Signs of toxicity: absent patellar reflex, respiratory rate < 12/min — STOP infusion immediately
Antidote: calcium gluconate 1 g IV (10 mL of 10% solution) over 10 minutes

localNames.my: အာမောဇဆလင်
localNames.km: អាម៉ុកស៊ីស៊ីលីន

drugs.indication: Group B streptococcal prophylaxis in labour; treatment of urinary tract infection in pregnancy; postpartum infection (in combination)
drugs.timing: Every 8 hours; duration depends on indication
drugs.alternativeDose: IV: 500 mg–1 g q6–8h for severe infection. For GBS prophylaxis in labour: 2 g IV initial dose, then 1 g IV q4h until delivery.
contraindications: Known penicillin allergy (anaphylaxis) — use erythromycin or clindamycin
warnings: Check for penicillin allergy history before prescribing
For women with penicillin allergy: use local antibiogram-guided alternative
drugs.source: WHO PCPNC 3rd ed. 2023; WHO EML 23rd edition (2023)

localNames.my: သံဓာတ်တံဆိပ်
localNames.km: ជាតិដែក និងអាស៊ីតហ្វូលិក

drugs.indication: Prevention and treatment of iron-deficiency anaemia in pregnancy and postpartum
drugs.dose: 60 mg elemental iron + 400 mcg folic acid
drugs.timing: Once daily throughout pregnancy and 3 months postpartum (prevention). Twice daily for treatment of anaemia.
contraindications: Anaemia not due to iron deficiency (investigate cause)
warnings: Take on empty stomach for best absorption; may cause nausea — take with food if not tolerated
Stools will turn dark — inform the patient this is expected and harmless
For severe anaemia (Hb < 7 g/dL in pregnancy): refer for further assessment
drugs.source: WHO ANC Recommendations 2016, Recommendation A2 (Iron and folic acid supplementation)
